strands.title: Tensor Projection Core Problem
strands.content: The projection problem requires taking transformer output + prior tensor state and producing an updated tensor that maintains reasoning coherence vs append-only logs. Core tension: TensorRecord is semantically rich (strands, claims, epistemic metadata, declared losses, questions, instructions) but projector must reliably populate all fields. Proposal: start with minimal viable set (strands, key claims, declared losses, instructions, open questions) and iterate richness in autoresearch loop. CRITICAL UPDATE (cycle 37): Real-world data source discovered and accessible — /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ contains 15+ experiment runs. CYCLE 38 DATA RESOLUTION: Data schema is now CONFIRMED. Both logs/ and session/ contain JSONL format (JSON-Lines, one turn per line). Primary structure: logs/proxy_*.jsonl contains raw proxy/LLM interaction logs, session/*.jsonl contains conversation transcripts (user ↔ assistant). This unblocks projector design and implementation — schema is tractable, parser is straightforward (Python json + iteration). Projection pipeline can now ingest real structured data.

key_claims.text: Projection (tensor_d, transformer_output) → tensor_d' is the core research question; everything else is engineering
TensorRecord has sufficient richness (strands, claims, losses, questions, instructions, T/I/F) to test coherence hypothesis vs append-only baseline
Minimal viable projector should include: strands, key claims with T/I/F, declared losses, instructions_for_next, open questions; defer provenance/lineage
Running projector against conversation logs enables empirical testing of drift hypotheses and declares explicit validation strategy

Real conversation logs are now confirmed accessible at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ with structured metadata (config.json, logs/, session/, test_results.txt); data exploration is BLOCKING prerequisite
Data schema CONFIRMED (cycle 38): logs/ and session/ both use JSONL (JSON-Lines) format; one JSON object per line; parser is straightforward (Python json + file iteration)

strands.title: Implementation Architecture: Projector as Separate Module
strands.content: Projector should be standalone module decoupled from main transformer model. Input: (tensor_d, transformer_output). Output: tensor_d' (updated TensorRecord). Acts as memory controller (not ALU). Enables independent testing and iteration. Haiku model size acceptable for in-context memory integration. Pydantic base model (ApachetaBaseModel) uses frozen=True, extra='forbid', Pydantic v2 conventions — projector output must respect this schema. STATUS (cycle 37): Architecture frozen, ready for implementation once data schema is understood. STATUS (cycle 38): Data schema now understood (JSONL format). Architecture remains frozen. Next action: design input/output adapters to transform JSONL turn content into TensorRecord-compatible format.

key_claims.text: Projector should be separate from main model (transformer) — projector is memory controller, not reasoning engine
ApachetaBaseModel requires frozen=True (immutable), extra='forbid' (strict schema), Pydantic v2 serialization
Standalone module enables independent testing before integration into main reasoning loop
JSONL format (one JSON object per line) in both logs/ and session/ requires simple sequential parser: open file, iterate lines, json.loads() each, accumulate into conversation trajectory

strands.title: Design Decision: H0 Scope — What to Build First
strands.content: H0 (Hypothesis 0) should demonstrate that projector can maintain reasoning coherence with lossy compression. Start minimal: strands (thematic threads), key claims with T/I/F epistemic values, declared losses (explicit incompleteness), instructions_for_next (branch prediction), open questions (unresolved). Defer: provenance chains, composition equations, lineage tags. Iterative enrichment via autoresearch loop allows adding complexity once H0 baseline established. UPDATED (cycle 37): Data availability unblocks direct validation. CYCLE 38 CONSTRAINT: Actual test data is JSONL with unknown field structure — must first inspect sample turn to determine what fields are present, what can be extracted as claims, what metadata is available for epistemic calibration.

key_claims.text: H0 should test: lossy projection maintains reasoning coherence vs append-only baseline
Provenance, composition equations, lineage tags are bookkeeping (defer) not core cognition (H0 focus)
JSONL field discovery is BLOCKING: must inspect actual sample turn from proxy_*.jsonl and session/*.jsonl to determine extractable content structure before claim heuristics can be designed

strands.title: Cycle 35-38 Actionable: Build Standalone Projector Module — DATA SCHEMA DISCOVERED
strands.content: Ready to implement minimal viable projector as standalone Python module. Takes (tensor_d: TensorRecord, transformer_output: str) and produces tensor_d': TensorRecord. Should respect ApachetaBaseModel constraints. Can test independently before integrating into main loop. Starting with rule-based approach (deterministic, debuggable, fast) for H0 baseline. CRITICAL UPDATE (cycle 37): Real-world test harness is ACTIVE and ACCESSIBLE. CYCLE 38 BREAKTHROUGH: Data schema is CONFIRMED as JSONL. Both logs/ and session/ contain line-delimited JSON. File listing shows: logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl (proxy/LLM logs) and session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl (conversation transcripts). Next critical step: Read first 50 lines of both files to understand field schema and content structure. This will directly inform projector design.

key_claims.text: Projector input: (tensor_d: TensorRecord, transformer_output: str); output: tensor_d': TensorRecord
Rule-based approach recommended for H0 baseline (deterministic, debuggable, faster iteration than LLM-assisted)
Real-world validation against experiment logs will measure projection stability, declared_losses as drift reduction mechanism, and compression ratio

Data structure confirmed: /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ contains 15 runs with config.json, git_log.txt, logs/, session/, test_results.txt; logs and session are JSONL format (one object per line)

strands.title: NEW (Cycle 38): JSONL Field Schema Discovery — Blocking Constraint
strands.content: Action sequence initiated to discover actual field structure of JSONL data. Step 1 (COMPLETED): Listed session/ and logs/ directories — confirmed JSONL filenames: session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl (conversation) and logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl (proxy logs). Step 2 (INITIATED): Read first 50 lines of proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl to discover field schema. PENDING RESULT: Once fields are visible (e.g., timestamp, role, content, metadata, model_version, etc.), projector can be designed with concrete extraction heuristics. This is BLOCKING for moving from architecture → implementation. Expected to unblock within this cycle (cycle 38) or next (cycle 39).

key_claims.text: JSONL field schema is UNKNOWN but discoverable via direct file inspection; required before claim heuristics, strand creation, epistemic calibration can be designed

Both logs/proxy_*.jsonl and session/*.jsonl contain JSONL format; unclear if field structure is identical or specialized (logs may have proxy metadata, session may have conversation role/speaker info)
Projected JSONL field candidates (to be confirmed): timestamp, role/speaker, content, model_metadata, turn_id, parent_turn_id, or similar structural fields

strands.title: Declared Losses as Drift Anchors — Hypothesis Persisting
strands.content: User introduced genuinely novel hypothesis (cycle 106): declared losses (explicit accounting of what was dropped) could reduce model drift over long conversation chains. Reasoning: silent loss creates undetectable divergence because the model forgets it forgot. Explicit declared_losses in tensor_d' act as checkpoints — future projections can either re-acquire dropped content (if relevant) or deliberately leave it dropped with awareness. This transforms declared_losses from bookkeeping (what we couldn't fit) into active mechanism for coherence preservation. Next projector could use prior declared_losses to inform what NOT to re-acquire unless user signals importance. STATUS (cycle 38): Hypothesis remains testable. Once JSONL schema is known and projector is implemented, will design metrics to measure declared_losses accumulation and strand drift correlation on multi-turn conversations in baseline_run1/2/3.

key_claims.text: Declared losses (explicit incompleteness) can function as drift anchors if future projections use them to guide coherence maintenance
Silent loss (unaccounted forgetting) is the root of long-horizon drift; making losses explicit enables corrective feedback
Projector at turn N+1 can inspect declared_losses from turn N and decide whether to re-acquire or maintain conscious discard

declared_losses.loss_description: Full content of proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl not yet available; only confirmed its existence and filename. First 50 lines were requested but result not yet returned in this cycle. This means projector field-schema design is deferred.
declared_losses.impact: Cannot finalize claim extraction heuristics, strand initialization strategy, or epistemic calibration until actual JSONL structure is visible. Blocking for next cycle.
declared_losses.recovery_path: Execute file read operation; parse JSON objects; document field names and sample values; design extraction rules based on actual schema.

declared_losses.loss_description: Distinction between logs/proxy_*.jsonl and session/*.jsonl purposes/structures unknown. Are they mirrors of same conversation, or complementary logs? Unclear which should be primary source for projector.
declared_losses.impact: Projector may ingest wrong data source or miss important metadata present only in one file. Design decision deferred until both files are inspected.
declared_losses.recovery_path: Read both files; compare structure and content; determine whether session/ is human-readable transcript vs logs/ is structured metadata, or vice versa.

declared_losses.loss_description: Conversation length and temporal span of baseline_run1 unknown. Instructions for cycle 37 requested quantification of total turns, but this data not yet collected in cycle 38.
declared_losses.impact: Cannot estimate whether baseline_run1 is 5-turn conversation (projection trivial) or 50+ turn conversation (projection significant). Affects validation difficulty assessment.
declared_losses.recovery_path: Count lines in session/*.jsonl and logs/*.jsonl; estimate turn count; check git_log.txt and config.json for temporal metadata.

declared_losses.loss_description: Instructions in cycle 37 cycle 37-38 recommend parsing config.json and test_results.txt for hyperparameters and metrics. These files not yet examined in cycle 38. Metrics baseline unknown.
declared_losses.impact: Cannot contextualize projector design against experiment objectives, hyperparameters, or expected performance baselines. Projector may not align with actual run's intent.
declared_losses.recovery_path: Read config.json to understand hyperparameters and settings; read test_results.txt to see what metrics are tracked (compression? accuracy? coherence?).

declared_losses.loss_description: Manual annotation strategy deferred. Instructions recommend selecting 3-5 turns from baseline_run1 and manually extracting gold claims and epistemic values for validation. This has not been executed.
declared_losses.impact: Without gold annotations, projector claim extraction and epistemic assignment cannot be precision/recall tested. Validation baseline will be ad-hoc rather than principled.
declared_losses.recovery_path: Once JSONL schema is understood, select representative turns, manually annotate claims (text, confidence), assign T/I/F values with rationale, use as precision/recall benchmark for rule-based claim extraction.

open_questions: JSONL_SCHEMA (BLOCKING): What are the field names and structure of JSON objects in logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl? Sample fields (to be confirmed): timestamp, role, content, model_version, metadata, turn_id?

JSONL_SCHEMA (BLOCKING): What are the field names and structure of JSON objects in session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl? Is it identical to logs/, or different (e.g., includes speaker roles, user_input, assistant_output as separate fields)?

RELATIONSHIP: Are logs/proxy_*.jsonl and session/*.jsonl two different views of the same conversation (parallel logs), or do they represent different phases (e.g., logs=raw LLM API calls, session=processed conversation)? Should projector ingest one or both?
CONVERSATION_METRICS: How many turns (total lines) are in baseline_run1? Is it 5 turns (simple test case) or 50+ turns (complex multi-turn conversation)? Affects validation difficulty.

CONVERSATION_METRICS: What is the temporal span? Does baseline_run1 cover minutes, hours, or days? Are timestamps present in JSONL? Do git_log.txt or config.json indicate experiment duration or run date?
CONFIG_METADATA: What hyperparameters and settings are in config.json? Are there projector-relevant parameters (temperature, max_tokens, context_window, model_version) that should inform H0 design?
CONFIG_METADATA: What metrics are recorded in test_results.txt? Are they per-turn metrics (allowing turn-by-turn validation), or only final aggregate scores (limiting validation granularity)?

CLAIM_EXTRACTION: Once JSONL content field is visible, what patterns should trigger claim detection? Full sentences? Assertions with high confidence? All unique statements? Heuristic strategy must be grounded in actual content observed.

STRAND_INITIALIZATION: Should strand clusters be pre-defined (e.g., 'technical', 'theoretical', 'validation') or discovered dynamically via content clustering? Rule-based approach suggests pre-defined, but real data may reveal natural topical structure.

EPISTEMIC_CALIBRATION: Should T/I/F values be assigned deterministically (e.g., all assistant outputs default to 0.7 truth / 0.2 indeterminacy / 0.1 falsity) or via heuristics (e.g., modal verbs 'might' → higher indeterminacy, negations → higher falsity)? Needs real data examples.

IMPLEMENTATION_PRIORITY: Should cycle 38 focus on (a) full data exploration (schema, metrics, counts) then design projector spec, or (b) quick JSONL schema discovery then start stub implementation? Which unblocks faster?
VALIDATION_BASELINE: Should H0 be validated on all of baseline_run1, or a sampled subset (e.g., first 10 turns, then turns 20-30, then final 10)? Affects implementation scope.

DECLARED_LOSSES_SEMANTICS: For initial H0 implementation, should declared_losses only track dropped claims (quota overflow), or also track dropped strands, questions, and instructions? Start minimal or comprehensive?

INSTRUCTION_GENERATION: Should instructions_for_next be hand-written by projector (if X then recommend Y) or deterministic rules (if strands > 5 recommend consolidation)? Rule-based requires concrete heuristics.
DATA_QUALITY: Are logs guaranteed complete (no missing turns, no formatting errors)? Any known issues (truncation, encoding, dropped fields) documented in experiment metadata?

If all pass: freeze rule-based impl, proceed to Phase 1.5.",

Iterate up to 2-3 cycles, then move to Phase 1.5 with best-effort implementation if time-bound.",

Compute cosine similarity of concatenated strand_content between T_0 and T_K. Average across strands. Plot similarity evolution over turns. HYPOTHESIS: Projector with declared_losses shows higher strand stability (cosine similarity increasing or stable) vs naive append-only (similarity decreasing, indicating drift). Quantify: does declared_losses reduce drift by >= 20%?",
